stem: The points (−2, 5), (3, −2), and (6, 4) lie in the xy-plane. What is the area, in square units, of the triangle formed by connecting the three points?
rationale: The correct answer is 25.5 (equivalent to 51/2). Using the shoelace formula, Area = (1/2)|x₁(y₂ − y₃) + x₂(y₃ − y₁) + x₃(y₁ − y₂)| = (1/2)|(−2)(−2 − 4) + 3(4 − 5) + 6(5 − (−2))| = (1/2)|12 − 3 + 42| = (1/2)(51) = 25.5 square units.
